observed_failure.surface: cross-platform contract compilation and final self-host execution
observed_failure.prior_local_claim: the v0.3.5 candidate had one portable source identity and affected-only validation began before any owner executed
observed_failure.observed_result: JSON Lines fixtures had different Windows and Linux hashes, the public self-host command executed declared owners before TestMesh froze an affected plan, and an operator-side process-name check briefly started a duplicate background owner before its complete descendant tree was terminated and verified absent

classification.owner: content projection, self-host claim boundary, and supervised background launcher
classification.error_signature: clean Linux contract hash differs from Windows, or an owner process starts before the frozen affected plan, or process liveness is checked through one executable name only

root_cause_backpropagation.supported_cause: JSON Lines was omitted from the canonical text suffix set; self-host combined claim creation with direct check execution; and the background operator initially inspected only one Python process name instead of the Windows process tree and command line
root_cause_backpropagation.would_have_failed_if: the model had required every maintained text suffix to prove LF/CRLF identity, required a zero-execution claim before TestMesh planning, and required process-tree identity rather than executable-name identity before any second launch
root_cause_backpropagation.new_model_item: canonical text identity covers JSON Lines; the public self-host path is claim-only; TestMesh alone executes will_execute owners; a background owner is identified and cleaned up by its complete descendant process tree
root_cause_backpropagation.owner_code_contract: claim current run with zero owners -> freeze exact TestMesh plan -> execute exactly will_execute owners once -> confirm zero descendants -> aggregate immutable receipts read-only

generalized_repair.rule: normalize line endings for declared maintained text formats only, keep binary files byte-exact, expose no public pre-plan execution route, and never infer background-owner uniqueness from one process executable name
generalized_repair.observed_regression: LF and CRLF JSON Lines compile to one source identity, self-host claim reports execution_count zero, and the final frozen owner run completed 46 of 46 owners with zero failed or not-run owners
generalized_repair.same_class_regression: binary normalization remains rejected, direct self-host execution is unavailable, the duplicate launcher tree was terminated with zero remaining descendants, and the post-router plan reused all 46 receipts with execution_count zero
generalized_repair.negative_boundary: do not add a compatibility reader, alternate self-host command, fallback launcher, Scheduled Task, unattended retry, or run-all reaction to an unmapped change

required_revalidation: JSON Lines LF/CRLF and binary byte-exact compiler regressions
FlowGuard validation-composition scenarios and known-bad cases
one frozen final TestMesh owner execution
transactional installed currentness and installation receipt replay
global-router refresh followed by receipt-only aggregation and read-only replay
clean Ubuntu and Windows GitHub Actions before v0.3.5 release

subjects: contract compiler portable JSON Lines identity

claim_boundary: This review validates the portable text identity, claim-first execution order, supervised process-tree cleanup, frozen full owner execution, installed binding, current global router, receipt-only aggregation, and read-only replay. Branch CI, immutable tag, GitHub Release, and unrelated maintenance units remain separate exact-commit gates.
